full_response:
The curve is defined by constant spatial coordinates \(r = r_0\), \(\theta = \theta_0\), \(\phi = \phi_0\) in the Schwarzschild metric, with \(r_0 > 2GM\). The only varying coordinate is \(t\), so the curve represents the worldline of a stationary observer. Parameterizing by proper time \(\tau\) yields the 4-velocity as the tangent vector.

The proper time interval is \(d\tau = \sqrt{1 - 2GM/r_0} \, dt\), so \(dt/d\tau = 1/\sqrt{1 - 2GM/r_0}\). Since \(dr/d\tau = d\theta/d\tau = d\phi/d\tau = 0\), the 4-velocity components are
\[
U^\mu = \left( \frac{1}{\sqrt{1 - \frac{2GM}{r_0}}}, 0, 0, 0 \right).
\]
This vector satisfies the normalization condition \(g_{\mu\nu} U^\mu U^\nu = -1\).

\boxed{U^\mu = \left( \frac{1}{\sqrt{1 - \frac{2GM}{r_0}}}, 0, 0, 0 \right)}

Confidence: 100%
